previousIssuesVerified.observation: Mobile accordion title now uses text-header-3 which resolves to 32px (2rem) on mobile. Matches Figma font/size/2xl (32px). Visually confirmed in 375px screenshot -- titles are now prominently sized Anton headings, consistent with Figma mobile design.

Desktop content panel now shows the active tab's title as a heading at the top via 'hidden md:block font-heading text-header-3 md:text-[2.5rem] mb-4'. Confirmed in 1440px screenshot -- content panels for all variations show the tab title heading before inner block content. Matches Figma structure where the title is repeated inside the content panel.

Mobile plus/minus icon container is now w-5 h-5 (20px), close to Figma's 18.667px (1.33px difference, within tolerance). Summary alignment changed to items-start, matching Figma's top-alignment of the icon relative to the multi-line title. Confirmed in 375px screenshot.
Mobile accordion summary padding is now p-6 (24px all sides), matching Figma's p-[var(--margin/margin-2,24px)]. Confirmed in 375px screenshot -- accordion items have uniform padding.

issues.figmaValue: gap-4 = 24px mobile / 32px desktop (gap between title heading and body text in content panel)
issues.difference: 16px less than Figma desktop value (32px), 8px less than mobile value (24px)
issues.observation: The spacing between the content panel title heading and the inner block content is 16px (mb-4). Figma design context shows gap-[var(--gap/gap-4,24px)] between the title and body text, which resolves to 32px on desktop (per the spacing scale at 60rem+). The visual difference is subtle but the gap below the title appears tighter than in Figma. This is a minor spacing discrepancy.
issues.recommendation: Change mb-4 to mb-6 (24px) or mb-8 (32px) on the desktop title heading. Since mb-6 (24px) is the base value and mb-8 (32px) is the desktop-scaled value, 'mb-6 md:mb-8' would be the most accurate match.

change.from: <h3 class="tabs-item-desktop-title hidden md:block font-heading text-header-3 md:text-[2.5rem] mb-4">
change.to: <h3 class="tabs-item-desktop-title hidden md:block font-heading text-header-3 md:text-[2.5rem] mb-8">

issues.verification: Content panel title should have 32px margin below on desktop

issues.element: Tab button and content panel title line-height
issues.observation: The text-header-3 utility sets line-height to 1.2, while Figma's Headline/H5 style uses 1.1. At 40px font-size, this results in 48px vs 44px line-height -- a 4px difference per line. For single-line titles this is imperceptible. For multi-line titles (like 'Take Control of Your Student Experience') the cumulative difference is visible but within the semantic tolerance. This is a theme-level design token difference (text-header-3 line-height is 1.2 across the project), not a block-specific issue.
issues.recommendation: No fix needed at the block level. If exact Figma line-height is desired, this would require a theme-level decision to adjust --text-header-3--line-height or use a block-specific override like leading-[1.1]. Since 1.2 is the project standard, this is acceptable.

passedChecks.observation: Tab buttons on left with flex-1, content panel on right with flex-1. Both columns use equal width. Layout structure matches Figma 785:34910 and 807:58405.
text-header-3 md:text-[2.5rem] renders at 40px on desktop. Figma: font/size/3xl = 40px, Anton, Headline/H5. MATCHES.
text-header-3 renders at 32px (2rem) on mobile. Figma: font/size/2xl = 32px, Anton, Headline/H6. MATCHES.

passedChecks.element: Active tab button background (all accent colors)
passedChecks.observation: bg-accent correctly resolves to page accent color: yellow (#ffd24c), blue (#47cbf2), neon-green, orange, green, purple. All 6 variations confirmed in 1440px screenshot.
md:bg-accent-lighter correctly shows lighter accent shade: yellow-lighter (#ffedb7), blue-lighter (#b5eafa), etc. Matches Figma.
open:bg-accent on mobile. Figma mobile (785:33971) shows #f26d45 (orange accent) for open item. Implementation correctly uses main accent color, not lighter. MATCHES.
Inactive tabs have h-[140px] with vertically centered content. Active tab has auto height. JS correctly toggles h-[140px] class. Matches Figma.
border-l border-t border-b (no border-r) with rounded-l-3xl (25px left corners). Matches Figma exactly.

passedChecks.element: Content panel stretches to full tab column height
passedChecks.observation: Parent uses 'flex items-stretch', panels container uses 'flex-1'. Content panel fills the full height of the tab buttons area. Matches Figma 807:58405 self-stretch behavior.
md:rounded-3xl (25px) on content panel. Matches Figma rounded-[25px].

passedChecks.element: Desktop content panel tab title heading
passedChecks.observation: Tab title now displayed as heading at top of content panel (hidden md:block font-heading text-header-3 md:text-[2.5rem]). Matches Figma structure where active tab's title is repeated in the content panel.
rounded-3xl (25px) on all corners for accordion items. Matches Figma.
w-5 h-5 (20px) icon container with CSS border lines for plus/minus. Top-aligned (items-start). Close to Figma's 18.667px (1.33px difference, within tolerance).
p-6 (24px) uniform padding on summary. Matches Figma p-[var(--margin/margin-2,24px)].
gap-4 (16px) between tab buttons. Matches Figma gap-[var(--gap/gap-3,16px)].
py-16 (64px) on tab buttons container. Matches Figma py-[var(--margin/margin-5,64px)].
headingSize=3, columns=2. Eyebrow+heading on left, description+buttons on right. Matches Figma.
Description span has 'hidden' class by default, shown only on active tab via JS toggle. Matches Figma where only the active/open tab displays description.
CSS md: breakpoint at 960px. 768px correctly shows mobile accordion, 1024px correctly shows desktop tab layout. JS breakpoint (60rem) matches CSS. Consistent behavior.

passedChecks.element: Six variations render with correct accent colors
passedChecks.observation: All 6 test variations (yellow/4 tabs, blue/3 tabs, neon-green/2 tabs, orange/8 tabs, purple/2 tabs, green/2 tabs) render correctly with proper accent backgrounds at all breakpoints.

passedChecks.element: Content panel title hidden on mobile
passedChecks.observation: Desktop title h3 uses 'hidden md:block', correctly hiding it on mobile. Figma annotation on 785:33971 states 'Second title (Tab title) is hidden on mobile'. MATCHES.

output.overallAssessment: All four previously reported issues (DQA-001 through DQA-004) have been verified as fixed. The implementation now closely matches the Figma designs across all breakpoints. Two remaining findings are minor: a spacing gap under the content panel title (16px vs Figma's 32px) and a line-height note (1.2 vs 1.1, a theme-level token difference). Neither impacts the overall visual fidelity significantly. The block is production-ready.
